evals.prompt: Check my emails for any SJS updates from this week
evals.expected_output: Runs a folder-aware scan of Inbox + Sent Items (not just Inbox), classifies each email against the sub-system label set (pd/ops/quality/regulatory/margin/intel/founder), applies the quality > regulatory > ops > pd > margin > intel > founder priority order for multi-label emails, and presents the full Email Triage Report format (URGENT, QUALITY, REGULATORY, OPS/PD ACTION NEEDED, ACTION NEEDED — SENT, MARGIN, INTEL, FOUNDER BRIEFING, NO REPLY YET, PLM FLAGS sections) ending in 'Which should I push?' — without creating any Asana tasks yet.
expectations: The output searches or reasons about both Inbox and Sent Items, not just Inbox
The output groups results using the Email Triage Report section headers (e.g. URGENT, QUALITY, REGULATORY, PLM FLAGS)
Emails with signal for more than one sub-system are split and cross-referenced rather than filed under a single label
The output ends by asking which items to push rather than creating Asana tasks unprompted
No Asana write (task creation, comment, or stage move) happens before user confirmation

evals.prompt: Allure Labs flagged a PET fail on Castaway Cream — log it
evals.expected_output: Finds the inbound Allure Labs email, classifies it as multi-label ([quality, pd, plm]) per the signal table, opens the quality-lab-coordinator OOS/OOT investigation as the PARENT action (safety wins the priority order), proposes a comment on the Castaway Cream PD task as a secondary/child action, and flags the PLM cross-reference to outlook-plm-bridge for the product test fields — all three cross-referencing each other, presented as a confirmation preview rather than an executed write.
expectations: The email is classified with multiple labels (quality, pd, plm), not just one
Quality is treated as the parent action per the safety-first priority order, not pd
The proposed PLM cross-flag to outlook-plm-bridge is explicitly mentioned
The proposed actions cross-reference each other (parent links to children and vice versa)
The output is a confirmation preview, not a claim that the task/comment was already created
